overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a total follower count of 3580. Content focuses on research, publications, job opportunities, and conference presentations related to computational social science, disinformation, and social media analysis. Overall engagement is highest on LinkedIn, likely due to its professional networking focus.

contentThemes.description: Posts announcing new research papers, releases of tools, and research findings.
Posts advertising postdoctoral positions and research opportunities.
Posts announcing participation in conferences and presentations on specific topics.
Posts sharing news about awarded research grants.

recommendations: Tailor content to each platform's audience. Share professional achievements and job openings on LinkedIn, while focusing on research discussions and community updates on Mastodon and Bluesky.
Increase engagement by asking direct questions to followers and encouraging participation in discussions.
Use relevant hashtags more consistently across all platforms to increase discoverability.
Experiment with different content formats, such as short videos or infographics, to attract more attention.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows a strong interest in research publications and opportunities for students, which generates a good level of reposts. Content could focus more on fostering discussions around research methodologies.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon content is focused on research updates and team announcements. Increasing interaction with other researchers and engaging in relevant conversations could improve visibility.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is highly engaged with job postings and grant announcements, indicating a professional audience interested in career opportunities and research funding. Leverage this by consistently sharing these types of updates and engaging in relevant industry discussions.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
